channel.title: Comments for page "The Musical Suspects"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Musical Suspects"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm reading this canon straight through, and let me tell you, pacing-wise, the last log entry was timed perfectly. A great in-world lift. Dunno if that was planned, or just a happy little accident, but it's sublime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just spent the last 20ish minutes putting together a Spotify playlist of all the Queen songs referenced in this tale, in order of appearance (possible memetic compulsive effect?)

Marvellous job, psul, great comic interlude in the Golgotha canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last log got me grinning like an idiot with all the Queen lyrics. I had already been enjoying this, but after that there was no way I couldn't dislike this.

+1
This is now the third MTF Eta-11 contest entry to clock up a +50 rating. 51 upvotes, and one "too goofy for Kalinin" downvote :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just spent a half hour reading all of the site's guidelines on member conduct and how to become a member just to upvote this. This is the best mission log I've read so far on this site.

Wow, thanks! There are a heap of good mission logs on the site, so I hope you find some more that you enjoy. Not very many of them use the format for comedy, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just spent a half hour reading all of the site's guidelines on member conduct and how to become a member just to upvote this. This is the best mission log I've read so far on this site. +1

Like a tiger! I'm gonna go, go, go…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the vote, and for the comments, LittleSallyDigby.

The use of PoI designations feels right for Foundation documentation, but I think you could've done more to remind the out-of-universe reader who you're talking about

I've added Charles Mace's name to the Phase 2 report - I've already linked to his other appearances (in SCP-1638 and Enjoy the Silence) in the text, so there's not much else to link to, but it's good to have a reminder, as Mace becomes important in Counterpoint, the next tale in the sequence.

The other two PoIs are newly created (I hesitate to call them characters, as they don't get any characterisation), so I don't have anything to link for them. I'd be delighted if anyone wanted to write some tales about them, however, and would definitely link to them in that case.

I've never seen the spelling "Freddy Mercury" outside of this tale- it's always been "Freddie"

Quite right - fixed.

You could just change the current link text to something like "for more information, see {Commander Richards's mission report}."

This is the trickiest for me. I didn't want to link to Enjoy the Silence as a mission report, as it's such a personal narrative for Hennessey. And in my headcanon, the reason that a report hasn't yet been filed is that the experience was emotional for all of them, and Commander Richards hasn't worked out how to file it. I've left it as "Post-engagement report awaited" for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three parts, all excellent. Upvoted.

Some notes:

  • The use of PoI designations feels right for Foundation documentation, but I think you could've done more to remind the out-of-universe reader who you're talking about. A quick parenthetical description of where the designation comes from, or encoding "PoI-XXXX" with a link to the document where the designation originates, would go a long way.
  • The pocket dimension in Theme and Variations felt like a video game I would like to play.
  • I don't know if this is a consensus thing or something the man himself had a preference on, but I've never seen the spelling "Freddy Mercury" outside of this tale- it's always been "Freddie."
  • I understand if you don't want to write logs for Elegy after giving it its own tale, but "no logs due to [the noise-canceling properties of the location]" feels a bit off. I'm certain the Foundation would at least debrief the task force afterward, if not actually come up with a way to monitor/record what was being "said" during the mission- conducting any operation without at least trying to record what happened is extremely out of character for the organization. You could just change the current link text to something like "for more information, see {Commander Richards's mission report}."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. That line was an awesome moment of wtf. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm embarrassed to say I only recognized about two-thirds of the Queen lines

(hangs head in shame).

Seriously, though, this was a great end to a great series. I think the change in tone worked, since the previous installment dealt entirely with the more serious threads. And it's nice for it to end on an up note. (And you don't get more "up" than than Queen's "we are the champions, of the world")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The goofy nature of this, compared with the rest of the entries in this series, was just too hard to swallow, personally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We have two, no three Freddy Mercurys coming from the barn.

And upvote.

Though for the first log, my thought was "Did they get stuck in Catherine?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess at the end of the day, all you need is love! *cough*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oops, I was just editing my comment above for clarity, but you posted before I finished.

Interesting. I hadn't realized it at the time, but now that you point it out I can see that much of the humor and fun indeed came from Mike. Keeping him for the end was a good move.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks very much for the kind words, Hercules. Much appreciated.

I wasn't expecting the Eta-11 contest arc to end in such a humorous way

I agree that the the team has been quite high on the angst-o-meter. It felt like they needed a bit of catharsis.

On that note, it's quite deliberate that the last part of the report is the first time in the whole arc where Mike Carter has been included in the (on-page) action. In my mind, his youth and enthusiasm is an important element in bringing the team together, and adds an element of hope for the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget that if you want to have a good time, just give him a call.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't stop this now, psul, I was having such a good time, I was having a ball! Beelzebub may have a devil put aside for me, but that won't stop me from upvoting. +1

I wasn't expecting the Eta-11 contest arc to end in such a humorous way, that was great - the first mission was pretty neat as well, I loved how inventive it was, but ultimately it's the last mission that steals the show (as I'm sure you meant it to). After all the feels and intensity of the previous entries, the goofiness of this one really came as a surprise, but it was a pleasant one - somehow, despite the change in tone from the rest of the series, this makes for a strangely appropriate ending, and an unexpectedly powerful one at that. And there's also the fact that you managed to make Eta-11 sing their every line without having the humor detract from their badassery. So, well done.

As an aside, the robots reminded me a lot of this for some reason , only with Freddie Mercury's face.

But seriously, I just want to say to the entire Eta-11 contest team (WrongJohnSilver, psul, Smapti, CumaeanSibyl) how much I enjoyed reading your entries. I really felt like I was reading a body of work sprung from a single mind, so much did each tale or article fit in perfectly with the others, and so much were the quality of the writing and the ideas consistently excellent; each entry added something to the whole, and none felt unnecessary, or somehow lesser than the others. The cross-linking was absolutely seamless, to the point where it felt like those skips you were referencing had been written for Eta-11. And the characters… I actually feel like I know them a little now, and I have a clear mental image of each of them in my head, which is saying a lot when you consider that this is short fiction. To say I'm looking forward to reading more about them would be an understatement.

Anyway, I just wanted to say congrats on your success, and good luck in the contest!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: and yes, I did sing along with the lyrics. I do love the ending though. So perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No need to apologise, and thanks for the vote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I forgot to upvote. Apologies, and that has been corrected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, this task force just gets better and better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Boom! Heck yeah thats what Im looking for! Almost lost confidence in the last post of this series but this brings it back!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my mind translated all the Queen lines into their correct tune

That's precisely what your mind should be doing. Perhaps it's not sufficiently clear, but the effect of the anomaly is that the MTF is having to sing all these lines, not just speak them. That's why Eta-11 is uniquely equipped to deal with this particular situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome post…

If not for the fact that my mind translated all the Queen lines into their correct tune.

Is there a thing like 'listening to too much Queen'? I think I still have some old cassettes around that probably have 'Fat Bottomed Girls in D Minor' on them from lying around too long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any mission where you can only communicate by quoting Queen is a good mission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mobile Task Force Eta-11 ("Savage Beasts") takes their bow for the 2016 MTF Contest. In this set of recovery logs and action reports, the MTF is on the hunt for the person who stole SCP-012 from containment, and goes into action against some musical anartists. Hopefully they can go out on a high note!

Many many thanks to the excellent Site-19 String Quartet, who have been a great team to work with. And a special thanks to the incomparable SunnyClockwork for Eta-11's logo, used here for the first time.

If you're new to MTF Eta-11, there are lots of cross-links in this article, but below is the recommended reading order for the contest entries:

  • A Convincing Lyre
  • An Impenitent Thief
  • Project Proposal 2014-2112: "Dreams of Failure"
  • SCP-2402
  • Enjoy the Silence
  • The Musical Suspects
